differences.title: 1. DATA MODEL
differences.leftDesc: Organizes data into predefined tables with fixed schemas for consistency and integrity.
differences.rightDesc: Stores data in flexible, JSON-like documents within collections, ideal for evolving data structures.

differences.title: 2. SCHEMA RIGIDITY
differences.leftDesc: Requires defining table structure before data insertion, ensuring data quality.
differences.rightDesc: Schema is not enforced at the database level, allowing for varied document structures.

differences.title: 3. SCALABILITY
differences.leftDesc: Traditionally scales well vertically by increasing server resources.
differences.rightDesc: Designed for horizontal scaling across multiple servers, handling massive datasets.

differences.title: 4. QUERYING
differences.leftDesc: Uses Structured Query Language (SQL) for powerful, complex data retrieval and manipulation.
differences.rightDesc: Employs its own query language, often document-centric and easier for simple lookups.

differences.title: 5. TRANSACTIONS
differences.leftDesc: Guarantees Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, and Durability for reliable transactions.
differences.rightDesc: Prioritizes availability and partition tolerance, with consistency achieved over time.

differences.title: 6. USE CASES
differences.leftDesc: Best for applications requiring complex relationships and strict data integrity.
differences.rightDesc: Ideal for content management, IoT, and applications with rapidly changing data.

differences.title: 7. JOIN OPERATIONS
differences.leftDesc: Efficiently handles JOIN operations between multiple tables due to relational design.
differences.rightDesc: Joins are typically performed at the application level or by embedding related data.
